title: amaryllis × recurviflora
intro: Amaryllis × recurviflora, also known as the belladonna lily or naked lady lily, is a type of flowering plant that is native to South Africa and is known for its trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant is said to have medicinal properties, including being used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions, due to its content of alkaloids such as scopolamine and hyoscyamine. In terms of health benefits, Amaryllis × recurviflora is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its roots or leaves, and can be found in the form of tinctures, teas, or capsules, although caution should be exercised when handling the plant due to its potential toxicity.
